# ![][image1]

# Challenge Track 3: Compliance Guardian

## Overview

Compliance review creates linear scaling problems. Every video requires manual screening against complex, region-specific rulesets. Review costs run $50-200 per hour of content. Delays extend time-to-market by days or weeks. Single violations risk $10K-$10M+ fines, platform removal, or brand damage.

Build an AI-powered compliance system that automates review while maintaining human oversight and regulatory auditability.

## Challenge Description

Create an intelligent compliance review system that ingests custom rules (e.g., *"Flag alcohol in content rated for under-21 audiences"*), analyzes video across visual, audio, and contextual dimensions, detects violations with precise timestamps, and surfaces findings with enough context for fast human validation.

**Critical requirement:** Your system must be explainable. When flagging content, provide specific reasoning (e.g., *"Detected beer bottle at 3:42 in scene with minors present"*) with evidence clips, not just confidence scores.

**Target workflows:**

* **Brand safety**: Screen advertising creative against brand guidelines before media buy  
* **Regulatory compliance**: Detect alcohol, tobacco, violence, or restricted content per broadcast standards  
* **Platform requirements**: Ensure content meets YouTube, TikTok, or streaming platform policies

## Technology Stack

### Video Understanding & Content Analysis (Required)

* **TwelveLabs Marengo \+ Pegasus via AWS Bedrock**: Use [Marengo](https://docs.twelvelabs.io/v1.3/docs/concepts/models/marengo) for object detection, scene understanding, and visual content classification. Use [Pegasus](https://docs.twelvelabs.io/v1.3/docs/concepts/models/pegasus) for audio transcription, dialogue analysis, and contextual reasoning. [Bedrock](https://aws.amazon.com/bedrock/twelvelabs/) provides unified access to both models with enterprise compliance and governance controls built in.  
* **AWS Bedrock credits** provided to all participants. Bedrock's responsible AI features include audit logging, content filtering, and explainability tools that align with regulatory compliance requirements.  
* **Recommended Resource**: [Automated Video Compliance Analysis with TwelveLabs Marengo and Pegasus](https://www.twelvelabs.io/whitepapers/automated-video-compliance-analysis)

### Application Infrastructure (Recommended)

* **Baseten** (Gold Sponsor): Deploy your compliance review system on Baseten for production-grade inference and workflow orchestration. Baseten handles model serving, API rate limiting, and multi-model coordination. Use Baseten to combine video understanding with rule-processing logic in a unified application layer.

### Remediation Workflows (Recommended)

* **LTX Video Generation** (Gold Sponsor): After detecting violations, use LTX for automated content remediation. Generate replacement shots, blur restricted elements, or create compliant alternatives. This extends compliance from detection to resolution. Use [this API key for credits](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1XWLZ395gOY3MQzQxQU0-w-6HI5YUuEVF7M1uXoPXOxI/edit?usp=sharing).

## Proposed Datasets

Consider using these footage:

1. **Advertising Creative** (20-30 pieces, 15-60 seconds each): Commercials with varying levels of compliance risk  
2. **Broadcast Content** (2 hours): Long-form content with embedded compliance challenges (alcohol, violence, language)  
3. **Compliance Rulesets**: 3 predefined scenarios (broadcast standards, brand guidelines, platform policies)

Here are example rules:

* *"No visible alcohol branding in content targeted to audiences under 21"*  
* *"Flag graphic violence or blood in content without proper rating disclosure"*  
* *"Detect unauthorized use of competitor brands or trademarks"*  
* *"Identify language that violates platform hate speech policies"*

## Evaluation Criteria

**Detection Accuracy (40%)**

* True positive rate vs. ground truth violations  
* False positive rate (incorrect flags wasting reviewer time)  
* Timestamp and scene boundary precision  
* Severity calibration (minor vs. critical violations)

**Explainability (30%)**

* Visual and audio evidence quality  
* Reasoning clarity for non-technical reviewers  
* Confidence transparency and uncertainty communication  
* Audit trail for regulatory reconstruction

**System Design (20%)**

* Rule flexibility (ease of adding or modifying compliance rules)  
* Multimodal analysis integration (visual objects \+ audio transcription \+ context)  
* Human review workflow (confirm, reject, annotate interface)  
* Processing throughput and production latency

**Production Readiness (10%)**

* API design for MAM/DAM or content management integration  
* Scalability to thousands of content hours daily  
* Edge case handling and graceful degradation  
* Security for sensitive content during analysis

## Submission Requirements

**1\. Functional Application** (required)

* Working demo accepting video uploads and custom compliance rules  
* Dashboard showing flagged content with timestamps, evidence clips, explanations  
* Human review interface with approve/reject/escalate workflow  
* 3-minute demo walking through 3 compliance scenarios

**2\. Technical Implementation** (required)

* Architecture diagram: rule processing and violation detection flow  
* TwelveLabs \+ AWS Bedrock integration details  
* Explainability approach: translating findings into human-readable explanations  
* Performance benchmarks: processing speed, accuracy, false positive rate

**3\. Compliance Report** (required)

* Per-ruleset accuracy metrics (precision, recall, F1)  
* Example flagged content with explanations  
* Comparison to manual review baseline  
* Discussion of ethical considerations and bias mitigation

**4\. Bonus Points**

* Multi-region compliance support (GDPR, FCC, international broadcast standards)  
* Continuous learning from human reviewer feedback  
* Risk scoring prioritizing high-severity violations  
* Integration with compliance workflows or ticketing systems
